title: Isnt It Sad How Anime Fans Now Havent Heard of EVA
display_title: Isnt It Sad How Anime Fans Now Havent Heard of EVA
wikitext:
[]: Isn't It Sad How Anime Fans Now Haven't Heard of EVA?             Quote Post by Kaworu-chan » Sun May 02, 2010 4:49 pm At my high school, there are anime fans (mainly freshman) who haven't even heard of Neon Genesis Evangelion and it's a classic! Is EVA really that old that the newer generation of anime fans don't even know about EVA?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun May 02, 2010 5:03 pm It's been over four years since Evangelion aired on Cartoon Network. Does it even still air on the network on occasion? Not to mention Evangelion is going out of print. Not to mention the new Evangelion films haven't (from what I can tell) brought over new blood the way the movies, the Platinum release, or the Cartoon Network airings did. I can see not having seen Eva, we can't have seen every anime ever. But never even heard of it? That's a bit of a stretch. Although I do concede, the show is getting old these days. It's over 15 years old now.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Sun May 02, 2010 5:06 pm It's not like Dragon Ball Z, Naruto or Bleach because they are provided by big American Dubbing companies presenting these shows on very popular television programs. ADV (now Section23Films), which distributes Evangelion, treats it as an under appreciated show. It's painful, yes I know, but that's a fact. In Japan, they go crazy over Evangelion. Though, with the religious symbols shown on the show (though really used as visual effects to change the story), some religious people in several regions of the countries might not want to present this program to anyone.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Sun May 02, 2010 6:12 pm I’m not really surprised that some newer anime fans have never heard of EVA. It seem like a lot of theses new fans don’t really care to watch anything that’s more then a few years old so naturally EVA would be unknown to them.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun May 02, 2010 6:26 pm You're mistaken on a few things. tomrule123 wrote: It's not like Dragon Ball Z, Naruto or Bleach because they are provided by big American Dubbing companies presenting these shows on very popular television programs.

[]:             Quote Post by planet news » Sun May 02, 2010 7:23 pm Eva will forever be on the Top-10 All-time lists, which will forever be the way that a lot of entry-level anime fans learn about anime.

[]: Re: Isn't It Sad How Anime Fans Now Haven't Heard of EVA?             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Sun May 02, 2010 7:41 pm Kaworu-chan wrote: At my high school, there are anime fans (mainly freshman) who haven't even heard of Neon Genesis Evangelion and it's a classic! Is EVA really that old that the newer generation of anime fans don't even know about EVA?

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Sun May 02, 2010 9:40 pm Kaworu-chan wrote: At my high school, there are anime fans (mainly freshman) who haven't even heard of Neon Genesis Evangelion and it's a classic! Is EVA really that old that the newer generation of anime fans don't even know about EVA?

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Sun May 02, 2010 10:36 pm I'm going out on a limb here, but I'd bet that significantly fewer kids these days have seen the original Star Wars than back in 1997 when it was re-released (and my dad stood in line in the freezing cold for five hours to get tickets for us). Simple matter of exposure.

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Mon May 03, 2010 1:05 am While EVA is popular, it's still no HST or Pokemon when comes to being popular in the USA. I'm not surprise just like most newer comic book readers never hear about Watchmen before the movie was release.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon May 03, 2010 2:04 am Being a recluse, I don't have much exposure to the younger crowd. But during my unsuccessful romp trying to help the local junior librarian out with the rowdy anime/manga group, I learned that, despite pretty much all of the kids being Adult Swim fags, none appeared to have seen NGE. But they sure loved their Death Note, Naruto, FMA, Bleach, and whatever else is popular with the American anime-watching middle/high school demographic these days. drinian wrote: I'm going out on a limb here, but I'd bet that significantly fewer kids these days have seen the original Star Wars than back in 1997 when it was re-released (and my dad stood in line in the freezing cold for five hours to get tickets for us). Simple matter of exposure.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon May 03, 2010 2:11 am The steady stream of "What the fuck did I just watch?" threads on /a/ indicate a healthy background awareness of the title .

[]:             Quote Post by Taekmkm » Mon May 03, 2010 3:49 am We're talking about real life.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon May 03, 2010 5:29 am Taekmkm wrote: We're talking about real life.

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Mon May 03, 2010 5:47 am I guess my exposure level was a lot higher than all of you... My teacher used Death and Rebirth as an example of Japanese avant-garde cinema, and a good portion of my film class had seen it on Adult Swim growing up. I'm the only one out of all of them whose spent ungodly amounts of hours rewatching and researching Eva, but IMO I wouldn't say that it's underexposed. It's also ADV/Section23's favorite title, isn't it? I don't think I've seen as many re-releases of one animu ever compared to Eva. The steady stream of "What the fuck did I just watch?" threads on /a/ indicate a healthy background awareness of the title .

[]:             Quote Post by JupiterCallisto » Mon May 03, 2010 7:17 am The Eva Monkey wrote: It's been over four years since Evangelion aired on Cartoon Network. Does it even still air on the network on occasion?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon May 03, 2010 8:29 am Guyver Spawn wrote: HST

[]:             Quote Post by JupiterCallisto » Mon May 03, 2010 9:14 am The Eva Monkey wrote: Just a bit. The Manga Entertainment release of Death & Rebirth and The End of Evangelion are starting to become difficult to come by, and I don't see Section 23 re-releasing the TV series anytime soon (they don't even have a website), so that will probably go out of print as well. Hell, even some of the manga is out of print, Volume 9 especially.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon May 03, 2010 10:00 am JKun76 wrote: Well section 23 is just starting up so it's understandable that they don't have a website.

[]:             Quote Post by Bloopy » Mon May 03, 2010 10:31 am child of Lilith wrote: I’m not really surprised that some newer anime fans have never heard of EVA. It seem like a lot of theses new fans don’t really care to watch anything that’s more then a few years old so naturally EVA would be unknown to them.

Is EVA really that old that the newer generation of anime fans don't even know about EVA?
